name: Úbeda, Canena, and Giribaile Reservoir Loop
name_original: Úbeda – vuelta por Castillo De Canena y Embalse Giribaile
description: This moderate 27.8-mile road cycling route from Ubeda features Castillo De Canena and scenic views of Embalse Giribaile.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Úbeda traverse a landscape characterized by the vast "sea of olive trees" in the province of Jaén, Andalusia. The city is situated on the Loma de Úbeda, a tableland between the Guadalquivir and Guadalimar river beds, providing varied elevation. The region offers a mix of well-paved surfaces and some challenging segments, with proximity to the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. This diverse terrain provides a compelling backdrop for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Úbeda The most popular road cycling route is Úbeda and Alto Guadalquivir Loop, a 33.6 miles (54.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route immerses riders in the diverse landscapes of the Alto Guadalquivir Natural Area. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ubeda – return via Jodar, a difficult 37.9 miles (61.1 km) path. This route involves significant elevation gain, passing through the town of Jódar. Local road cyclists also love the Úbeda, Canena, and Giribaile Reservoir Loop, a 27.8 miles (44.8 km) trail leading through the Embalse de Giribaile and past Castillo De Canena, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Úbeda is defined by undulating olive groves, views of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, and the Embalse de Giribaile. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Úbeda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 9 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Úbeda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Úbeda?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Úbeda, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 19 moderate routes and 39 challenging routes, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Úbeda?
A: The mild weather during &lt;strong&gt;spring (April to June)&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;autumn (September to November)&lt;/strong&gt; makes these seasons ideal for exploring Úbeda and its surroundings by bike. During these months, you can enjoy comfortable temperatures and the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While many routes around Úbeda are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy route available. Additionally, several of the 19 moderate routes could be suitable for cyclists with some experience looking for a less strenuous ride. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996067620/ubeda-canena-and-giribaile-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda, Canena, and Giribaile Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 44.8 km long.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on a road cycling route in Úbeda?
A: Road cycling in Úbeda offers breathtaking views of the vast 'sea of olive trees' that defines the Jaén province. You'll also encounter the undulating terrain of the Loma de Úbeda, and many routes provide scenic vistas of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, and the Embalse de Giribaile (Giribaile Reservoir).

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Úbeda offers several challenging routes for experienced cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996069922/ubeda-return-via-jodar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ubeda – return via Jodar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 61.1 km path with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009141872/ubeda-and-alto-guadalquivir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda and Alto Guadalquivir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 54.1 km route that immerses riders in diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Úbeda?
A: The road cycling routes in Úbeda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique experience of riding through the 'sea of olive trees,' the varied terrain, and the scenic views of natural parks and reservoirs.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Úbeda?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Úbeda are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996067620/ubeda-canena-and-giribaile-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda, Canena, and Giribaile Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009141872/ubeda-and-alto-guadalquivir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda and Alto Guadalquivir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering a complete circular experience.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: You can integrate historical landmarks into your cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996067620/ubeda-canena-and-giribaile-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda, Canena, and Giribaile Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by Castillo De Canena. Other notable historical sites in the region include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Chapel of the Savior&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pablo Church in Úbeda&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored before or after your ride.

Q: Are there any routes that feature water bodies or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The Embalse de Giribaile (Giribaile Reservoir) is a prominent feature on several routes, offering beautiful waterside views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996067620/ubeda-canena-and-giribaile-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda, Canena, and Giribaile Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading directly through this scenic area. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4982802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic viewpoints of the region?
A: Yes, the elevated position of Úbeda on the Loma de Úbeda, combined with the vast olive groves, provides numerous opportunities for panoramic views. While specific viewpoints are not always marked on cycling routes, the undulating terrain ensures many sections offer sweeping vistas of the 'sea of olive trees' and distant mountain ranges like the Sierra Mágina. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Guadalquivir River and Giribaile Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight offering such a perspective.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that venture into natural parks?
A: Yes, some routes provide access to or views of the region's natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009141872/ubeda-and-alto-guadalquivir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda and Alto Guadalquivir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, immerses riders in the diverse landscapes of the Alto Guadalquivir Natural Area. The Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park is also nearby, and while not directly traversed by all road cycling routes, its presence shapes the scenic backdrop of the region.
tips:
This monumental bullring was inaugurated in 1857 where there was an old convent known as San Nicasio, founded at the beginning of the 15th century. 
The inauguration date was that year of 1857 in which the bullfighter "Lagartijo" took the alternative, the same one who later inaugurated the Baeza bullring.

Small town near Úbeda and Baeza. A beautiful Castle dominated the hillside, built in the times of the Calatrava order. Be sure to try the famous Premium and Gourmet Olive Oil brand from Castillo de Canena during your visit.
Water source in the town of Canena. Don't forget to fill your water bottle. Or jump on a hot day.

The Giribaile reservoir is located on the slopes of the La Lambra area and was built between 1993 and 1997 to collect water from the Mar de La Loma river. The water from the reservoir submerged the 16th century Ariza bridge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ubeda - return via Castillo De Canena and Embalse Giribaile route?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a moderate road cycling route. While primarily designed for road bikes, some segments may be unpaved, offering a varied surface. The region of Andalusia is known for its undulating terrain and challenging climbs, so expect some notable ascents requiring a good level of fitness.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate and requires a good level of fitness due to its length and elevation gain. While not extremely difficult, beginners might find some of the climbs challenging, especially if they are not accustomed to longer distances or varied terrain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass by the impressive Castillo De Canena, the Caño Fountain of Canena, and enjoy scenic views of the Giribaile Reservoir and Submerged Ruins. Starting or finishing in Úbeda, you might also see the Plaza de Toros de Úbeda.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists can expect to complete this 44.8 km route in about 2 hours and 20 minutes. However, this duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the pace you maintain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route in Ubeda?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Úbeda, as a town, generally offers various parking options. It's advisable to look for public parking facilities or street parking near the starting point of the route in Úbeda.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Andalusia region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, so if cycling then, an early morning start is recommended to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near towns like Canena, where you might find local establishments. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the moderate difficulty and potential for challenging sections.

faqs.question: Is the Ubeda - return via Castillo De Canena and Embalse Giribaile route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while dogs are generally not suitable companions for race bike rides, if you are considering a different type of bike or a shorter section, local regulations for dogs in public spaces and near historical sites should be checked. For the full route, it's not recommended for dogs.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route. You can enjoy the scenic roads and points of interest freely.

faqs.question: What kind of gear do I need for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given it's a racebike route with potential unpaved sections, a well-maintained road bike is essential. Consider tires that can handle varied surfaces if you anticipate encountering unpaved segments. Always bring a helmet, water bottles, basic repair kit, and appropriate cycling attire for the weather conditions.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de Guadalupe, Camino de la Carretera de Córdoba, and Camino Alto de Cuesta Zapata, among others.
